text: Medicine & Health Sciences Kattankulathur - Chennai Home Medicine & Health Sciences Excellence in Medical Education The SRM Medical College Hospital and Research Centre is situated on the NH 45 GST road at Potheri between Tambaram and Chengalpattu in a sprawling 33.61-acre campus. The proximity of the Medical College Hospital campus to other Institutions of the SRM Institute of Science and Technology namely Engineering, Management, Dental, Arts & Science and Biotechnology is strategic in facilitating collaborative research. Mission To make available preventive, curative, and rehabilitative medical care to everyone at minimal cost To teach clinical skills to medical and health science graduates and post graduates while performing focused clinical research for ‘Bench to Bedside’ application Medical College and Hospital Research Centre Dr. Nitin M Nagarkar Pro Vice-Chancellor (Medical) Pro VC's Message The medical world is full of miracles. But behind every medical miracle lie the keen eyes and the steady hands of a medical professional. Here, at the SRM Medical & Health Sciences Faculty, you will constantly push the limits, exploring new ways to combat diseases and life threatening ailments. As a medical student, you will need to build a solid foundation while also staying in tune with the latest medical trends. SRM’s Medical & Health Sciences Faculty consists of a variety of specialized departments. Each has its own library along with a laboratory and a museum. One of the few institutions to have a full-fledged multi-specialty hospital on campus, the faculty equips you with the latest and best tools for providing primary, secondary and tertiary care. I extend a warm welcome to SRM Medical & Health Sciences. Chairs/Deans and HoDs Explore our Programs Students seeking inter-disciplinary programmes and flexibility in course curriculum will find just that at SRMIST. A student shall have an option of choosing any one of the following pathways (i) Major (ii) Major with Specialization (iii) Major with Minor or Major with specialization and a Minor The core philosophy that is practiced in SRMIST as part of teaching-learning process is Inter Disciplinary Experiential Active Learning (IDEAL) Find Programs Programs Offered We provide treatments across 52 departments including Superspeciality departments like Cardiology, Nephrology, Urology, Neurosurgery etc. It is the largest hospital in Chengalpattu district and can serve over 3,00,000 in and around Kattankulanthur. The hospital is a brilliant platform to change the world for better by offering excellent treatment while improving the careers of our healthcare experts.

National Service Scheme (NSS) The ideals of Mahatma Gandhi provide inspiration for the NSS’s ideological stance. “NOT ME, BUT YOU” is the NSS motto. Home National Service Scheme (NSS) NSS Overview National Service Scheme (NSS) is a widely recognized and influential youth-centric program in colleges across the country. NSS was established in 1969, the year Mahatma Gandhi celebrated his birth, with the primary goal of fostering the character and personality of student youth via volunteer community service. The ideals of Mahatma Gandhi provide inspiration for the NSS’s ideological stance. “NOT ME, BUT YOU” is the NSS motto. NSS was commissioned by SRM Institute of Science and Technology in 2003. The activities of the SRMIST NSS mainly focus on Community Development Services, Social Services, Environmental Awareness Programs, Health Awareness Programs and Technology Transfer Programs. The NSS at SRMIST has guidelines to conduct various Regular and Special Camping activities. Apart from social activities, SRMIST NSS Cell offers NSS Course 21GNM102L to Engineering students during their first-year course of study. Every Semester around 800 students take up the course and gets benefitted. Prof.T. Lakshmanan Advisor / NSS CELL Advisor's Message As the Advisor of the NSS Cell, I take this opportunity to share some important information and updates regarding our activities and initiatives. The NSS is a platform that encourages civic involvement, social responsibility, and personal growth among our students. We firmly believe in the ability of service to effect holistic transformation. We work to improve the world for everyone as a group. We regularly engage in activities and programs designed to address a range of social concerns and improve the well-being of our communities. These programs include a wide range of topics, such as efforts to promote good health and hygiene, educational support programs, and environmental conservation activities. Serving the community benefits people in need while also fostering leadership potential, social responsibility, and Personal development among the students. The Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports GOI’s National level training and development programmes are another thing I strongly advise you to utilize. These programs are designed to improve your abilities, broaden your knowledge, improve your communication skills, open up your uniqueness towards servicing the society. I would like to remind everyone to stay in touch with our NSS team so they can be informed of the most recent news, activities, and volunteer opportunities. Our dedicated team is here to support you and provide guidance throughout your journey with the NSS. Small acts of kindness can ignite a flame of change in our communities. Together, let’s be the spark that lights up lives and creates a brighter future for all. NSS Programme Officers NSS Annual Reports NSS Course - Activity Book Activities and Initiatives Achievements Events Dr. Prakash V Programme Coordinator / NSS CELL Coordinator's Message National Service Scheme (NSS) is a voluntary organization for young students that plays a crucial role in shaping character and fostering social responsibility to serve society without bias.

Together, let’s be the spark that lights up lives and creates a brighter future for all. NSS Programme Officers NSS Annual Reports NSS Course - Activity Book Activities and Initiatives Achievements Events Dr. Prakash V Programme Coordinator / NSS CELL Coordinator's Message National Service Scheme (NSS) is a voluntary organization for young students that plays a crucial role in shaping character and fostering social responsibility to serve society without bias. It aims to inculcate social welfare in students, and through community services, it helps in building their personality. By engaging in various community service activities, NSS aims to instill a sense of empathy, compassion, and commitment to social welfare among students. Overall, the diverse range of regular activities undertaken by NSS volunteers demonstrates their unwavering commitment to serving the community and making a positive impact on society, contributing to building a better world for all. NSS volunteers work to provide help in natural and man-made disasters by supplying food, clothing, and first aid training programmes, arrange blood donation camps to fulfil the shortage of blood requirements in hospitals, engage in tree plantation to protect the environment and save Mother Earth, participate in cleanliness campaigns for hygiene and a healthy life, conduct road safety awareness campaigns to reduce road accidents, and organize street plays and awareness campaigns to save the girl child and educate her etc. NSS Special Camps serve as transformative experiences that not only broaden volunteers’ perspectives but also equip them with essential life skills and values. By immersing themselves in rural communities and actively engaging in service-oriented activities, volunteers emerge with a heightened sense of social responsibility and a deeper understanding of the principles of value education. With all the experiences from NSS, I am sure that volunteers will learn how to lead a life of dignity with a positive mindset. NSS provides volunteers with a transformative experience that not only enhances their personal growth and sense of responsibility but also equips them with the skills and values needed to contribute meaningfully to the nation’s development. By fostering a culture of service, leadership, and citizenship, NSS empowers volunteers to lead lives of dignity and make lasting contributions to the progress and prosperity of their country. with Warm Regards, Dr. Prakash V Motto of NSS The Motto of NSS “Not Me But You”, reflects the essence of democratic living and upholds the need for self-less service. NSS helps the students development & appreciation to other person’s point of view and also show consideration towards other living beings. The philosophy of the NSS is a good doctrine in this motto, which underlines on the belief that the welfare of an individual is ultimately dependent on the welfare of the society as a whole and therefore, the NSS volunteers shall strive for the well-being of the society. Objectives of NSS The SRMIST NSS programme in colleges operates with a set of well-defined objectives.

The philosophy of the NSS is a good doctrine in this motto, which underlines on the belief that the welfare of an individual is ultimately dependent on the welfare of the society as a whole and therefore, the NSS volunteers shall strive for the well-being of the society. Objectives of NSS The SRMIST NSS programme in colleges operates with a set of well-defined objectives. These include: Community Service: Encouraging students to actively engage in community service activities, addressing social issues such as education, healthcare, environmental conservation, rural development, and more. Personality Development: Providing a platform for students to develop leadership skills, enhance interpersonal communication, and build a strong ethical foundation through various training programs and workshops. Empathy and Social Awareness: Promoting a sense of empathy, compassion, and understanding towards the needs and challenges faced by marginalized sections of society, encouraging students to become responsible citizens. Centre for Statistics Home Research Research Wings Centre for Statistics Overview Centre for Statistics is a hub of statistical training, research, and consultancy at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur. Our aim is to unite multidisciplinary students, research scholars, and faculty to foster innovative thinking and collaboration, as well as address new research challenges across various disciplines within our institution. As a leader in statistical applications and data science, the Centre represents a significant step forward for our institution. Vision Pursue Excellence in Statistical Training and Research Serve as an Epicenter for Statistical Activities within the Institute Develop a Dynamic Partnership Between Academia and Industry Promote the Use of Statistics Across Various Fields Mission To make numbers speak and transform data into actionable insights, for advancing statistical knowledge and contributing to the development of academic research and industrial collaborations. 2000 Established 100 Consultancy Services 1 Training Programs 1 Publications 1 Funded Projects Professor and Head Email: Welcome to Centre for Statistics The field of statistics is increasingly essential in today’s world due to its vital role in fostering evidence-based decision-making. This discipline supports decision-makers, policymakers, researchers, government and private agencies in reshaping society through informed choices. With the advent of modern computing and technological advancements, the importance and application of statistics have expanded significantly, benefiting students and practitioners alike. Centre for Statistics in SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur demonstrates the intent to benefit the student and scholar community, and faculty members with world-class assistance in this subject. In addition to helping students acquire data analytic skills, the centre provides in-depth knowledge regarding the application of statistical techniques through effective supervisory training programs. Also to push forward the frontiers of knowledge by offering statistical consultancies and interacting with industries. We encourage students, research scholars, and faculty members to utilise the resources and expertise available at the Centre for Statistics. You can find us in Room No.1303, 13th Floor, University Building. For any inquiries or further information, please write to us at. Meet Our Staff Events Projects Programs Basic Statistics using SPSS (Trial Version) Advanced Statistics for Research Introduction to Systematic review & Meta-analysis using R Survey Design Methodology Bio-Statistics using SPSS (Trial Version) Statistical Cell Statistical Training Unit The Statistical Training Unit at the Centre for Statistics offers training programs and short courses designed to enhance statistical knowledge and skills. We provide tailored sessions for internal and external participants, including faculty, researchers, and students. Our focus is on delivering high-quality, practical knowledge to all levels of proficiency. Data Analytic Unit Our Data Analytic Unit specializes in translating complex research questions into clear statistical problems. We work closely with researchers to provide precise and actionable insights through advanced statistical analysis and methodological expertise. Our goal is to support a wide range of research needs through interdisciplinary collaboration and expert consultation. Statistical Consultancy Unit The Statistical Consultancy Unit offers professional statistical services on a fee-for-service basis. We work closely with researchers to provide precise and actionable insights through advanced statistical analysis and methodological expertise. Our goal is to support a wide range of research needs through interdisciplinary collaboration and expert consultation. Statistical Consultancy Unit The Statistical Consultancy Unit offers professional statistical services on a fee-for-service basis. We assist with grant writing, questionnaire design, survey methodology, data analysis, result interpretation, and report writing. Our services are aimed at providing precise and impactful solutions to support your research and data needs. Analytics Reach US Head & Professor of Statistics Room 1303, 13th floor, University Building, SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur, Chengalpattu Department Mail Id: “For Prior appointments please contact Executive Assistant, Udhaya Kumar.Contact Number – 8072297359” Where could your journey at SRMIST take you? text: SRM College of Occupational Therapy Home Medicine & Health Sciences SRM College of Occupational Therapy About Programs Placements Faculty Infrastructure Contact About the College Occupational Therapy is a healthcare profession that enables people of all ages to be independent in their day-to-day activities. SRM College of Occupational Therapy was started in the academic year 1996 under the Tamil Nadu Dr. M.G.R. Medical University. In the year 2004, the college was brought under the ambit of SRM University, and the same year Postgraduate program was started. SRM College is the only occupational therapy college to offer BOT, MOT, and PhD programs in Tamil Nadu. It is one of the colleges in South India which is recognized by the All India Occupational Therapists’ Association (AIOTA), the National Governing body of the profession and World Federation of Occupational Therapists (WFOT), the apex professional body worldwide, and accredited by NAAC with graded A++. These recognitions give a distinct advantage of being accepted at par in the world of medicine, which allows students to seek higher studies or employment in abroad. Occupational Therapy in SRM is committed to guiding our students through a distinct learning process that allows the individual student to think, evaluate their own learning styles and experience learning through civic engagement in emerging practice areas and service-learning opportunities. We provide our students with exceptional opportunities to learn and grow as they transition from the classroom to successful careers in Occupational Therapy. 100% Job placement is guaranteed for students who graduate every year in BOT and MOT. 80% of our graduates are well placed in countries like USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Singapore, and Middle East. We have well-equipped clinical setup & research facilities. Tie up facilities for clinical training is being provided in major corporate hospitals such as SIMS Hospital, Vadapalani and other organizations such as NIEPMD at Muttukadu, Right Hospital, MDA – a special school for Dyslexia. Students of SRM College of Occupational Therapy have been placed in overseas for clinical placement at Latrobe University, Australia. Campus placement is done for USA & India through our SRM placement cell for major hospitals and companies. Community outreach program is carried out in villages in and around Kattankulathur where our faculty and students conduct Disability Survey and Health Education to provide Occupational Therapy intervention at doorstep.

Ganapathy Sankar Dean Welcome message Welcome to the College of Occupational Therapy at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur campus. The College of Occupational Therapy commenced in 1996, we have numerous achievements to rejoice. Our faculty is committed in educating future leaders, generating new knowledge, improving individuals health and well-being, and serving the greater community Locally, Nationally and Internationally. We also partner with prominent scientists and Healthcare professionals in India and around the world to shape the future of the profession in an everchanging Healthcare environment. Our reputation and influence are growing through the outstanding scholarship and advocacy of our distinguished faculty and staff. The faculty and staff of the College of Occupational Therapy mentor and inspire our students to think, care, create, and pursue justice in our world. We do this by preparing the next generation of leaders to practice innovative, evidence-based healthcare for our diverse global community. Our students are fully engaged throughout the learning process in their in-person and virtual classrooms, through interprofessional patient scenarios in our simulation facility, clinics and research labs, community outreach and advocacy. We are dedicated in preparing students for contemporary healthcare practice, including an interprofessional, team-based approach to person-centered care. I hope you will explore our website to discover your future path in healthcare. High Performance Computing Center (HPCC) Home Research HPCC Overview A High-Performance Computing Center is a location with a High-performance Computers (HPC) facility usually needed for research work. A high-performance computer (HPC) is a computer with a high-level computational capacity compared to a general-purpose computer. The performance of High-performance computers (HPC) is measured in floating-point operations per second (FLOPS) instead of million instructions per second (MIPS). In general thousands of dedicated processors are placed in proximity to each other (e.g. in a computer cluster); this saves considerable time moving data around and makes it possible for the processors to work together (rather than on separate tasks), for example in mesh and hypercube architectures. High-performance computers (HPC) are typically used for scientific and engineering applications that must handle very large databases or do a great amount of computation (or both). Core Research Areas Computational Materials Science Computational Chemistry Big-Data Analysis and Data Science Genetic Engineering Atmospheric Science Computational Mechanics and Fluid Dynamics Who can use Any faculty, members and students of SRMIST can potentially use this High-performance Computing facility, based on their needs. Training Program, creating e-learning database and series of hands-on session to develop computational skill among the students and faculties of the university. REACH REACH (Center for Research in Environment, Sustainability Advocacy and Climate CHange) was initiated at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur, Tamil Nadu with the desire to support and enhance a cohesive university-wide environment of research excellence where faculty are inspired NRC The Nanotechnology Research Center (NRC) with its state-of-the-art facilities is committed to the responsible development of nanotechnology as an important part of its environmental, health and safety research strategy. CACR The Center for Advanced Concrete Research (CACR) at SRM University was started in the year 2010, to carry out interdisciplinary research in the field of concrete, a most common material of construction in any built environment and a basic necessity for accelerated economic development. SRM-DBT Platform SRM Institute of Science and Technology in collaboration with Department of Biotechnology, Govt. of India has established this advanced research facility under the project “ SRM-DBT Partnership Platform for Contemporary Research Services and Skill Development in Advanced Life Science Technologies Medical Research Centre SRMIST’s aMRC established in 2008 promotes research in medical & allied sciences.

SRM-DBT Platform SRM Institute of Science and Technology in collaboration with Department of Biotechnology, Govt. of India has established this advanced research facility under the project “ SRM-DBT Partnership Platform for Contemporary Research Services and Skill Development in Advanced Life Science Technologies Medical Research Centre SRMIST’s aMRC established in 2008 promotes research in medical & allied sciences. In addition to helping PhDs Researchers, MRC among other things also mentors the faculty and the students in Scientific enquiry. Center for Statistics SRMIST’s Centre for Statistics is the hub of Statistical Training, Research and Consultancy and it It brings together students, research scholars, and faculty to bring fresh thinking and collaboration. Earthquake Research Cell Earthquake Research cell (ERC) established in 2012 to decipher causes for origin, occurrence and prediction of Earthquake by better understanding the geodynamical behavior of this natural disaster on the globe. HPCC Supercomputer center is a location with a supercomputer facility usually need for research work. The supercomputer is a computer with a high-level computational capacity compared to a general-purpose computer. Performance of a supercomputer is measured in floating-point operations per second (FLOPS) instead of million instructions per second (MIPS). SCIF SCIF portal consolidates the state-of-the-art research facilities available under the Directorate of Research in SRMIST and allows users to directly request & reserve time for use. text: Notable Alumni Home Alumni Affairs Notable Alumni All Colleges Faculty of Engineering & Technology Faculty of Science & Humanities Medicine & Health Sciences College of Agriculture Sciences SRM School of Law Faculty of Management We are proud of our alumni Business Leader Mr. Ravi Kundru CEO - Ventech Solutions and QurHealth Ravi Kunduru is the CEO of Ventech Solutions and QurHealth, leading both companies with a focus on innovation and growth. At Ventech Solutions, he drives technology solutions that optimize business operations across industries. With QurHealth, Kunduru focuses on transforming healthcare through digital health solutions and improving patient outcomes. His leadership is centered around fostering innovation, enhancing operational efficiencies, and leveraging technology to address industry challenges. Kunduru has a strong background in technology and business management, helping both companies expand their impact in their respective sectors. He is passionate about harnessing the power of digital transformation to create value for clients and communities. B.E (CSE) Year: 1995 Business Leader Mr. Sriram Krishnan Senior Policy Advisor for Artificial Intelligence @ The White House Sriram Krishnan is a Senior Policy Advisor for Artificial Intelligence (AI) at the White House. He advises on AI policy, focusing on ensuring its development aligns with ethical standards, public welfare, and security. Krishnan plays a key role in shaping government strategies related to AI governance, regulation, and its impact on society. Before joining the White House, he held leadership positions in the tech industry, collaborating with companies, regulators, and think tanks. His work addresses critical challenges like AI ethics, bias, data privacy, and the economic implications of AI. Krishnan bridges the gap between technology innovation and policy-making, guiding the U.S. AI strategy. BTech. (IT) Year: 2005 Alumni Profile All Colleges select search Public Service & Politics Shri. Ashok Kumar Malhotra Mission Director - National Technical Textiles Mission Shri Ashok Kumar Malhotra, Mission Director of the National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM), leads India’s initiative to advance technical textiles under the Ministry of Textiles. With expertise in Textile Technology, AI, and Management, he has spearheaded over 168 research projects worth ₹510 crore, fostering industry-academia collaboration. His work emphasizes applications in healthcare, geotextiles, automotive, defence, aerospace, and sports. Under his leadership, NTTM has established sector-specific Centres of Excellence, including SITRA for medical textiles and ATIRA for composites. Shri Malhotra also promotes exports through partnerships with MATEXIL and aligns Indian products with global standards. His efforts focus on building a complete ecosystem, integrating manufacturing, research, and skill development. By addressing challenges such as low domestic penetration of technical textiles, he aims to position India as a global leader. Shri Malhotra envisions India becoming the third-largest technical textiles market by 2047, driven by innovation-led growth and strategic international collaborations. At Ventech Solutions, he drives technology solutions that optimize business operations across industries.

B.E (CSE) Year: 1995 Business Leader Ms. Sheela Kunduru Founder & President, Sritech Global Inc. Mrs. Sheela Kunduru is the founder & President, Sritech Global Inc. She has been actively working to develop and improve various processes and projects within the government. B.E (CSE) Year: 1995 Business Leader Dr. Sudha Jamthe Author: Generative AI @ Business School of AI Sudha Jamthe is an author and expert in Generative AI, currently teaching at the Business School of AI. She is passionate about the transformative potential of AI technologies and has authored books that explore how generative AI is reshaping industries. Jamthe’s work focuses on helping professionals and organizations understand the practical applications of AI, especially in business contexts. She is an advocate for AI education and its role in fostering innovation across sectors. With a background in technology and entrepreneurship, Sudha has a deep understanding of both the technical and business aspects of AI. Her insights guide leaders and learners to harness AI for growth and efficiency. B.E (CSE) Year: 1999 Business Leader Mr. Ashok Ramachandran Group CEO, REE Corporation Ashok Ramachandran is a seasoned global business leader and Group CEO of REE Corporation, Vietnam, leading a diversified billion-dollar conglomerate across Energy, Water, EPC, Air Conditioning, and Real Estate. An ET 40 Under Forty awardee, he is known for driving strategy, transformation, and end-to-end P&L leadership across mature and emerging markets. With over two decades of international experience, he has held senior leadership roles, including COO & Board Member at JSW Energy, President & CEO of Schindler India, and Managing Director for Schindler in Vietnam, Malaysia, and Brunei, as well as regional leadership roles in Australia. An engineering professional by training, Ashok specializes in change management, negotiation, talent development, and operational excellence, and is an active LinkedIn thought leader sharing personal views on leadership and success. B.E (Instrumentation & Control) Year: 2002 Business Leader Dr. Ojas Mahapatra CEO, FABRUM Ojas Mahapatra, a tech business leader and CEO of FABRUM in New Zealand, holds a B.Tech in Biotechnology from SRM University. Originally from India, he pursued a PhD. in Nanotechnology from the University of Canterbury on a prestigious MacDiarmid Institute scholarship.

Originally from India, he pursued a PhD. in Nanotechnology from the University of Canterbury on a prestigious MacDiarmid Institute scholarship. Recognized as one of New Zealand’s top emerging executives, Ojas was awarded a Prime Minister’s Business Scholarship and honed his leadership skills at London Business School. Before FABRUM, he led InsituGen, a professional athlete’s drug-testing company. B. Tech (Biotech) Year: 2003 Business Leader Mr. Sriram Krishnan Senior Policy Advisor for Artificial Intelligence @ The White House Sriram Krishnan is a Senior Policy Advisor for Artificial Intelligence (AI) at the White House. He advises on AI policy, focusing on ensuring its development aligns with ethical standards, public welfare, and security. (IT) Year: 2005 Civil Service Vikraman Vasudevan, IPS Deputy Inspector General of Police @ NIA Vikraman Vasudevan is the Deputy Inspector General of Police (DIG) at the National Investigation Agency (NIA), where he plays a crucial role in overseeing national security and counter-terrorism operations. With extensive experience in law enforcement, he is involved in leading investigations related to terrorism, organized crime, and national security threats. Vasudevan’s work focuses on strengthening intelligence networks, coordinating with various agencies, and ensuring the safety of the nation. He has a proven track record in handling high-profile cases and ensuring justice in complex investigations. As a senior officer at the NIA, Vasudevan is committed to upholding the law and maintaining peace in the country. His leadership and strategic insights are vital in tackling security challenges at both domestic and international levels. B.Tech (CSE) Year: 2005 Artist Mr. Sam Anton Indian film director and screenwriter Mr. Sam Anton, a renowned film director and screenwriter who pursued a BTech. in CSE from SRM Institute of Science and Technology in the year 2012. He has made a mark in Tamil cinema with four successful films, starting with the horror comedy “Darling” in 2015. His recent hits include “Enakku Innoru Per Irukku” (2016), “Nooru” (2019), and “Gurkha” (2019). Sam Anton’s cinematic journey continues to captivate audiences. B. Tech. (CSE) Year: 2007 Business Leader Mr. Nikhil Chaudhary Nutritionist CEO, Nikhil Chaudhary Wellness Dr. Nikhil Chaudhary is a renowned nutritionist and the CEO of Nikhil Chaudhary Wellness, where he leads efforts to promote holistic health through personalized nutrition plans. With a deep understanding of nutrition science, he helps individuals achieve optimal health and wellness by tailoring diets to their specific needs. Dr. Chaudhary is passionate about educating people on the importance of balanced nutrition in improving overall quality of life. His approach blends modern science with natural wellness practices. With a deep understanding of nutrition science, he helps individuals achieve optimal health and wellness by tailoring diets to their specific needs. Dr. Chaudhary is passionate about educating people on the importance of balanced nutrition in improving overall quality of life. His approach blends modern science with natural wellness practices. As a wellness advocate, he provides guidance on sustainable lifestyle changes to prevent chronic diseases. Dr. Chaudhary is recognized for his expertise in diet management and its role in mental and physical health. His work extends to helping clients, organizations, and communities adopt healthier habits for long-term wellness. B.Tech (Biotech) Year: 2009

International Hostel Home SRM Hostels International Hostel Our Hostels International students come in huge numbers to SRM for its outstanding learning environment that fulfills their academic aspirations, intellectual passion and the desire to experience cultural diversity at its richest. There are 2 blocks allotted for International students with a capacity of around 400. The hostel block is completely air-conditioned. NRI Premium International Hostel – Boys This exclusive international hostel accommodates men and women students respectively on triple sharing basis. All rooms in this hostel block are air-conditioned. Email: Green Pearl International Hostel Outside the campus, we are having 1 more International Hostel named Green Pearl International Hostel within 10 mins walk from the campus, which is service apartment type of accommodation will be provided. Shuttle buses are available from Hostel to campus for easy commutation of students.. For the benefit of the international students, the hostel also offers multi-cuisine dining facility providing Thai, Chinese and Indian foods. Tamil Nadu Governor Thiru R. N. Ravi Calls Youth ‘National Assets’ at SRMIST’s Indian Republic @ 75 Conclave Home Tamil Nadu Governor Thiru R. N. Ravi Calls Youth ‘National Assets’ at SRMIST’s Indian Republic @ 75 Conclave SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) commemorated 75 years of the Indian Republic through the Conclave on Indian Republic @ 75 and the inauguration of Vendhar Museum – Phase II, dedicated to the Constitution of India, at its Kattankulathur campus on 8th January 2026. The programme brought together academic leadership, students, and distinguished dignitaries to reflect on India’s constitutional values, democratic institutions, and the role of youth in nation-building. The event was presided over by Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar, Founder Chancellor, SRMIST, and was graced by Thiru R. N. Ravi, Hon’ble Governor of Tamil Nadu, as the Chief Guest. The programme commenced with the National Anthem, followed by Tamil Thai Vazhthu and the singing of Vande Mataram, marking 150 years of the national song that played a pivotal role in India’s freedom movement. Following the conclave, the Hon’ble Governor inaugurated Vendhar Museum – Phase II, which features curated exhibits on the Constitution of India, its evolution, foundational principles, and democratic institutions. The museum underscores SRMIST’s sustained commitment to constitutional literacy, civic responsibility, and experiential learning. Addressing students and faculty, Thiru R. N. Ravi, Hon’ble Governor of Tamil Nadu, observed that “today, India is the world’s largest, robustly functioning and stable democracy,” describing it as an achievement that should instil pride in every citizen. Highlighting the civilisational roots of Indian democracy, he stated, “Democracy has been in our DNA. It has been a way of life of our people in Bharat for several thousand years.” He further noted that “over the last 75 years, our democratic institutions have grown stronger and more robust.” Emphasising the role of educational institutions in nurturing responsible citizens, the Governor remarked that SRM Institute of Science and Technology “not only imparts academic knowledge, practical knowledge, and skills, but also introduces students to our cultural and national values.” Urging students to recognise the opportunities before them, the Governor said, “This is the golden time for Bharat. Idhuve neram, ponnaana neram.” Stressing the importance of youth in shaping the nation’s future, he reminded them, “Each one of you is a national asset. When you grow to your fullest potential, the nation grows automatically.” He encouraged students to dream big, work with discipline and perseverance, and actively contribute to the vision of Viksit Bharat. Presiding over the function, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar, Founder Chancellor, SRMIST, spoke on the critical role of strong academic institutions in sustaining democracy and national progress. “Running a university with dignity, safety, and academic responsibility is a challenging task, and it requires autonomy, commitment, and mutual respect,” he said. Highlighting the need for institutional empowerment, Dr. Paarivendhar underscored that adequate funding and academic autonomy are essential to maintain academic standards and research excellence. “Universities must be empowered to pursue excellence without compromise. Highlighting the need for institutional empowerment, Dr. Paarivendhar underscored that adequate funding and academic autonomy are essential to maintain academic standards and research excellence. “Universities must be empowered to pursue excellence without compromise. Sustained funding and academic autonomy are essential to safeguard the future of higher education,” he stated. Referring to Vendhar Museum – Phase II, the Chancellor noted that the initiative reflects SRMIST’s commitment to preserving constitutional values and educating future generations about the foundations of the Republic through immersive and experiential learning. “We have always celebrated national milestones in this university because education must strengthen unity, national consciousness, and responsibility,” he added. Prof. C. Muthamizhchelvan, Vice-Chancellor, SRMIST, described the occasion as one of deep national and institutional significance. “Today marks a moment of profound pride as we commemorate 75 years of the Indian Republic and inaugurate Vendhar Museum – Phase II, dedicated to the Constitution of India—the moral and legal framework that sustains our democracy,” he said. He further noted that the museum reflects the Founder Chancellor’s vision that education must go beyond academics to shape civic consciousness and ethical responsibility. “As a university, we take pride in fostering graduates who combine knowledge with integrity and patriotism with responsibility,” he added. The Conclave on Indian Republic @ 75 concluded with a renewed affirmation of India’s democratic ideals and the vital role of youth and academic institutions in nation-building. With the inauguration of Vendhar Museum – Phase II, SRM Institute of Science and Technology reiterated its commitment to constitutional awareness, civic engagement, and value-based education, in support of a progressive and developed Bharat. Directorate of Sports At SRMIST, sports are a vital part of holistic education, promoting fitness, teamwork, and excellence. Guided by Founder Chancellor Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar’s vision, SRMIST provides world-class sports facilities, professional coaching, and opportunities for athletes to shine at national and international levels. Home Sports At SRMIST, sports are an essential part of holistic education and student life. Guided by the vision of our Founder Chancellor, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar, SRMIST aims to nurture excellence both in academics and athletics. The University offers world-class sports facilities including spacious playgrounds, indoor stadiums, fitness centers, and the state-of-the-art Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar Aquatic Complex. With professional coaching, modern infrastructure, and structured training programs, SRMIST encourages students to excel in national and international tournaments. Annual intercollegiate events and intramural competitions foster teamwork, leadership, and sportsmanship across all disciplines. Through Sports Quota admissions and scholarships, talented athletes are recognized and supported to achieve their dreams. At SRMIST, sports are not just recreational activities—they are a way of life that builds discipline, determination, and character, truly reflecting the Founder Chancellor’s vision of empowering youth through education and sport. SRMIST Champions in Action: December Month Medals Count 0 Gold 0 Silver 0 Bronze 0 State 0 South India 0 National 0 International Monthly Achievements Prof. R. Mohana Krishnan E-Mail: Phone: +91-44-2741-7281 Director - Sports Sports play an important role in every human being’s day-to-day life and bring people together irrespective of their age, gender and nationality. Our Founder Chancellor has a great vision to make SRMIST as a Temple of Sports in India. Directorate of Sports is marching towards fulfilling his vision as a reality. Over the last few years, SRMIST has seen a lot of success like never before. Young achievers in various sports have won prizes and medals at various state, National and international level events. Our goal is to increase the participation of our teams in various competitions and win medals and bring laurels to the University and the country. SRMIST is one of the few Universities in the country which encourages sports and games which has changed the lives of many young sports men and women. I strongly believe that sports help to inculcate teamwork, sacrifice, discipline and leadership qualities. In addition to the regular sports activities, the Directorate of Sports, SRMIST offers a variety of courses through the Department of Physical Education & Sports Sciences and the Department of Yoga.

Rahul Becomes India’s 91st Grandmaster Sports Achievements A Golden Finish at the Telangana India International Challenge 2025 Sports Achievements SRMIST Claims Seven Medals at the 18th Tamil Nadu State Sub Junior Archery Championship Sports Achievements Pedalling with Purpose: SRMIST Cyclists Shine at HCL Cyclothon 2025 International Standard Swimming Pool SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) boasts an international standard swimming pool at its Kattankulathur campus. This Olympic-sized facility, measuring 50 meters in length, is equipped with multiple lanes and advanced filtration systems to ensure a top-notch swimming experience. It serves as a hub for competitive training, recreational activities, and campus events, promoting a culture of fitness and well-being among students. With safety personnel on-site, the pool offers a secure environment for both professional athletes and casual swimmers. Dr. T.R.P Aquatic Complex Outdoor Games Most popular sports like basketball, vollyball, football, cricket, tennis, kabbadi, badminton are played by a large number of students in the evenings as well as early morning in our beautiful sprawling outdoor grounds and courts. Indoor Stadiums Indoor sports activity is vibrant and our students have access to a vast range of indoor sports like Table Tennis, Shuttle, and other fitness activitis.

Dr. T.R.P Aquatic Complex Outdoor Games Most popular sports like basketball, vollyball, football, cricket, tennis, kabbadi, badminton are played by a large number of students in the evenings as well as early morning in our beautiful sprawling outdoor grounds and courts. Indoor Stadiums Indoor sports activity is vibrant and our students have access to a vast range of indoor sports like Table Tennis, Shuttle, and other fitness activitis. Fitness Center For students to stay fit and healthy, all hostels are equipped with gym and fitness centre facilities – treadmill, exercise bikes and more of the latest fitness equipment. The central gym for men at SRM is also equipped with body building equipment. All gyms and fitness centers are open both in the morning and evening hours. A healthy body and a healthy mind with Yoga 100s of students and staff of SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) participated in the virtual celebration of International Day of Yoga. Large scale Yoga programs are held in the many facilities within the large sprawling campus of SRMIST including in its multipurpose indoor stadium, lawns, playgrounds, auditoriums. Renowned guests and Yoga specialists often visit our campus every year to help our students and staff master the ancient Indian gift to the world’s wellbeing and health. There is also students run Yoga club within SRMIST Sporting Achievements SRM Institute of Science and Technology values the inspiration and accomplishments that sports enable to our students and our institution. It is with this unflinching conviction that we are dedicated to keep scaling up our facilities, support, funding and commitment towards excellence in sports. We don’t believe in living in the past laurels alone. We take them as proof of our potential for the future. Our constant and consistent victories of our students in various championships across the nation and internationally keep us forge ahead in this direction to foster excellence. It is our dedicated champion coaches, support staff with the SRMIST’s Leadership who bring out the best out of our hard working and committed sportspersons/students. To get admission under the Sports Quota, SRMIST holds trials at its Kattankulathur campus. Those who are seeking to join Engineering, Management, Science and Humanities, B.Sc., Physical Education, Diploma Yoga & MSc Yoga, Law, Hotel Management and Health Sciences, through Sports Quota can take part in these trials after registering themselves in the prescribed online registration form that is announced during admissions season each year. SRM Central Instrumentation Facility (SCIF) Home Research SCIF Overview SRMIST is continually investing and setting up laboratories in addition to enhancing the lab equipments and softwares on a regular basis. As a measure of its commitment to creating meaningful and society uplifting impact, SRMIST is open to collaborating with anybody within SRMIST as well as externally among the industry and academic segment. You are welcome to access this SCIF portal that consolidates the state-of-the-art research facilities available under the Directorate of Research in SRMIST. You may directly put through your request to us to successfully reserve your access to certain facility / equipment at SRMIST. Dean (Research) The Head, SRM Central Instrumentation Facility Email:. Welcome to SCIF The SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) for over 3 decades now invested in bringing best of the learning and research enablers to its students, scholars and faculty members. SRMIST’s legacy of research excellence and impact has made a mark in the society at large and especially among the industry and government. Faculty members are actively encouraged to undertake high value, world-class research projects from various government and private sector. SRMIST given its legacy and scale of operations is ever so open to contributing to research endeavors of anybody within SRMIST or externally. It gives a great platform for our faculty members and scholars to be of assistance to other fellow researchers from the industry or academia. We welcome you to benefit from the ecosystem SRMIST offers to you and send us your request for our support. We will be more than happy to collaborate with you in so far as offering our facilities and equipments for your research endeavors. Our SCIF wishes you the very best in your research and innovation efforts. Meet Our Staff Research Facilities at the Centre Center for Environmental Nuclear Research has the independent laboratory facilities with state-of-art equipment for its research work. Facilities Service Charges & Guidelines Book Instruments Reach Us The Head, SRM Central Instrumentation Facility, Directorate of Research, RRP-910, Sir C. V. Raman Research Park, SRM Institute of Science and Technology, SRM Nagar, Kattankulathur – 603 203 Chengalpattu District, Tamil Nadu, India. For general queries write to Where could your journey at SRMIST take you? Placements Hostels About Us

Directorate of Online and Distance Education (DODE) Home Directorate of Online and Distance Education (DODE) The Directorate of Online and Distance Education (DODE) at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) is dedicated to providing students with flexible and accessible learning opportunities. Through innovative online platforms and distance learning methodologies, DODE offers a wide range of programs that cater to the diverse needs of learners across the globe. Our mission is to extend SRMIST’s academic excellence to those who seek quality education beyond traditional classroom settings. With a robust curriculum designed by industry experts and experienced faculty, we ensure that students gain both theoretical knowledge and practical skills, preparing them for successful careers in their chosen fields. Dr. Manoranjan Pon. Ram Director - Directorate of Online and Distance Education (DODE) Dr. Manoranjan Pon. Ram is an eminent professional and educationist who has been modernizing and revitalizing the digital facet of higher education. In his career span of over 24 years, he has been associated with various government universities PAN India projects, to develop LMS compatible Interactive learning audio-visual materials, student engagement strategies, online assessment and content delivery system. He is an evangelist of Flipped Learning pedagogy and has done extensive research on Flipped Classroom model and Blended mode of learning. Author is another adjective used for this former member of editorial boards of various leading journals who has eight edited books on Management and IT to his credit. On the educational front, Dr. Manoranjan Pon. Ram has achieved diverse academic qualifications such as Bachelor’s degree in Engineering and Post-Graduation in Management with a doctorate in E-Commerce makes a strategic combination of Management and Information Technology. He has received the prestigious awards such as “Rastriya Gaurav Award”, “Certificate of Excellence” from honorable Dr. Bhima Narain Singh – former Governor of Tamil Nadu & Assam for meritorious service in Management and IT Education and “Udyog Shiksha Sahyog Puruskar”. Online Education Distance Education

text: Student Life Home Life at SRM Student Life Student Life SRMIST’s student life not only enables a great education – something which lasts a lifetime. Beyond that, the range of opportunities allows students to determine her/his student life at SRMIST. To put it in simple terms, Student Life can be what a student wants it to be. At the end of their student phase at SRMIST, we want our students to have the self-belief to be whoever they want to be, and to feel confident and excited about their future. Getting around the Campus Walking and cycling are the most popular ways to get around the SRM campus. And with state-of-the-art security and 24 hour emergency services, it’s the safest place to be. The SRM group runs a fleet of buses for transporting students to and from the city and to other areas. Public transport buses and trains also ply to points near the campuses. Campus Lifestyle It’s a diverse community of students at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University), and the activities on offer are as diverse and varied too. From entertainment to extra-curricular or even religious pursuits, there’s never a dull moment on campus. There’s never a dull moment on campus, as the activities on offer are as diverse and varied too – from entertainment to extra-curricular or even religious pursuits. Student Affairs All activity related to extracurricular programs, forums, clubs, community service, and entrepreneurship cell and students council are managed by the student affairs department. Counselors are available to guide students and share personal issues. Explore More Clubs Several clubs are active on the SRMIST campus, initiated by enthusiastic students. The Robotics Club is a good example, motivating students, distributing low cost kits, conducting workshops and organizing competitions. Explore Exciting opportunities, the way you want to be, choices are yours All major Indian festivals are celebrated in the campus with complete enthusiasm and enjoyment. Lushing lunches are explicitly made in students’ hostels to mark these festivities and to give the students a taste of the region’s culinary traditions. Housing & Dining Community Centers Diversity Religious Life

Earthquake Research Cell (EQRC) Home Research Research Wings EQRC Overview SRMIST established an Earthquake Research cell (EQRC) in July 2012 for Advanced Research on the causes for origin, occurrence and prediction of Earthquake by better understanding the geodynamical behavior of this natural disaster on the globe. Further to establish the non-plate tectonics causes for the earthquake through re-looking into the existing causes and to develop computer simulation model for geodynamic character, prediction, prevention and management model to reduce the damages on the Earth. Vision To be a Global Centre of Excellence in Earthquake Research, Disaster Management and Mitigation studies Mission To be a leading Centre for Earthquake Research of international repute for the investigation of Geodynamic activity. To enhance the knowledge based on research, education and information transfer to reduce the damages through systematic understanding of seismological characters. To provide capacity building to mitigate future earthquake scenario for better planning and development. Dr. R. Sivakumar Co-ordinator Phone: 044 - 27455510, 47437500 Email: Welcome to EQRC SRMIST is among the first few institutions in the country to establish the Earthquake Research Centre, thanks to the foresight of our Founder & Chancellor Dr. T R Paarivendhar. Our Centre’s focus in terms of geographic regions are Sikkim – Darjeeling, Himalaya region in the North East, Andaman Region (islands in the Bay of Bengal), Ponnair Basin area in the South and Gujarat in the West. We have 12 themes that we are actively pursuing at EQRC, SRMIST. The Researchers are senior and tenured specialists in various practice areas who contribute to the furthering of our research activity. Our students and scholars who get to work at our EQRC stand to benefit immensely from our senior Faculty Members’ guidance and applied (on-the-field) learning. At EQRC, it is our endeavor to accomplish breakthrough research and contribute new ideas, concepts and solutions. Dr. S. S. Sridhar Professor, Department of Computing Technologies, SRMIST Research Theme Our research themes allow us to build on our existing strengths and invest in novel complementary areas. EQRC’s advantage is that our research is not confined to our center and there is a sizable proportion of our centre’s activity involving work on-field. Major themes of our research are as follows: Seismic hazard assessment Active tectonic study - Identification of Active fault zones. Geomorphic and morphotectonic anomaly- analysis of geomorphic indices, paleo-seismic evidences Crustal deformation studies and development of deformation model and 3D visualization. Analysis of epicenter data and prediction through ANN model. Quantification of seismic energy and simulation model Mathematical model through continuum / regression model. Remote sensing based surface fault mapping and geotectonic analysis. GIS based seismic database creation. Development of customized Seismic Information System. Designing seismic resistant structures. Disaster planning and Management Focused Areas Sikkim- Darjeeling Himalaya, N-E region Andaman Region Ponnair basin, South India Gujarat, western India Research Highlights Facilities Our world-class facilities make SRMIST one of the India’s best universities for research and study.

Development of customized Seismic Information System. Designing seismic resistant structures. Disaster planning and Management Focused Areas Sikkim- Darjeeling Himalaya, N-E region Andaman Region Ponnair basin, South India Gujarat, western India Research Highlights Facilities Our world-class facilities make SRMIST one of the India’s best universities for research and study. SRMIST is lead by the visionary Founder and Chancellor who has embedded research orientation and have supported us through investing in a wide range of facilities and resources to support our cutting-edge research. Whether it is subscription to internationally important knowledge inputs and reference material to state of the art labs, we have it all at our EQRC. Corporate Organizations and academics are invited to make use of our facilities and research services, including using our labs and equipment and consultancy services. Following is a selection of our research facilities Facilities Publications & Patents The experiential insights and research outcomes are often carried for publication in the nationally and internationally renouned Journals. Our researchers get to submit and present papers among the specialists from across the world on specialized topics connected to Earthquake Research. SRMIST is a platform for anybody seeking to create a dent through their commitment and hard work as we can count on some of the most valuable and best research ecosystem. This automatically means there are so many opportunities to pick, prepare and submit papers. We are proud that our faculty members and alumni have published book-chapters and books that are often reference literature to the current day students and research scholars worldwide. An indicative list of some of these publications and patents are mentioned here in this section. Industry Connect Home Research Industry Connect Industry Connect Back in 2003, SRM set up an International Advisory Board (IAB) consisting of 31 Distinguished Deans, Directors and professors from top universities across the world. SRM’s IAB is the first across the Indian University system. IAB works towards helping SRMIST set Global Standards and Internationalize through bringing globally acclaimed curriculum, research and teaching methodology to its students and faculty members. Now over 50+ Members are on IAB and they are from the best of the universities across the world including Harvard Business School, Kennedy Public School (Harvard University), MIT, Stanford, LSE, UC Berkeley, Cambridge, NUS among others. Testimonials The Semester Abroad Program has greatly helped me improve and sharpen not just my academic skills but has also benefited my future career prospects and has given me the right direction to streamline my efforts. Getting accepted for Masters in Computer Science at University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee, after completing a year as a Transfer Student is a great example in itself for the success of the Semester Abroad Program at SRMIST. Also, Being offered a Project Assistant ship position this Summer'09 to Financially Support my Studies and gain Knowledge with Research, amounts to the Huge Success of SAP Omar Mehta SAP Transfer to University of Wisconsin, USA "Coming to Warwick as a part of the Dual Degree Programme was perhaps the best decision I ever made! I shall be getting both my B.Tech and M.Sc degrees in just 4 years, ahead of my peers. And, we will be allowed to work 2 years after graduation here. Warwick has the best research labs and many eminent scientists - and has opened up exciting opportunities for SRMites." Sanjana (Genetic Engg) at Warwick, UK "I am excited to have got transferred to the University of Wisconsin, Milwaukee at the end of my third year at SRM. Will be completing my BS and MS at the US in just two years, saving cost and time. Many thanks for opening up this world of opportunity for me. I never dreamt about this, even a year ago." Taran Bedi (EEE) at UWM, USA Where could your journey at university take you? Academics Research Placements

Home Career Centre Gateway To Great Careers Career Centre is the centre point of career development, interpersonal skills and placement activities. Career Centre is a target recruiting ground for all major recruiters and has strong linkages with industry and is highly regarded by employers. The Career Centre in SRM Institute of Science and Technology plays a vital role in making SRM a favoured destination for many national and international organizations to recruit fresh talent nurtured at the Institute. Continuous institute-industry interaction, Pre-Joining learning initiatives, participating in industrial exhibitions, fairs, seminars to promote useful interaction with the industries and alumni activities are some of the initiatives of Placement Office. The excellent infrastructure of the Career Centre facilitates activities like pre-placement talks, written tests, group discussions, and interviews as per the requirements of the recruiting organizations. *2025 Batch (As on 03rd January 2026) 1 + Number of Roles + Offers + High Paying Offers LPA Highest CTC 0 LPA Average CTC Dr. N.Venkata Sastry Director, Career Centre T: +91-44 -27455774 (D) / 27452270 Extn: 7301 Director's Message The Career Centre at SRMIST encompasses the Placement Office and the Career Development Centre. Career Development Centre helps to identify the interests and capabilities of students in relation to their career goals and academic progression. Students are encouraged to pursue their passion. Intensive coaching and training is provided in personality development and in shaping their leadership qualities. The Placement Office besides, placement and training holds workshops, events like Hackathons, Quiz etc., conferences, talks by expert, internship, industrial visits to enable students to be up-to-date with contemporary trends and be ‘Market Ready’ by the time of graduation. We are ably supported and guided by our Founder & Chancellor, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar, who in his inimitable spirit of service has set for us a benchmark of tradition of excellence. We are therefore able to initiate some of the firsts in the Indian higher education system. For instance, our CDC collaborates with Career Center to focus on raising employability of our students. I look forward to personally seeing you in our campus and satiate your leadership talent needs. Dr. Paarivendhar Research Colloquium Monday, 21 April, 2025 Wednesday, 23 April, 2025 Home Events Dr. Paarivendhar Research Colloquium SRMIST - Dr. Paarivendhar Research Colloquium About SRM Institute of Science and Technology has been organizing Dr. Paarivendhar Research Colloquium annually, a unique platform to foster inter-disciplinary research and encourage research scholars of the Institution to present their research findings to the peer community. The objective of the colloquium is to provide an exclusive opportunity for research scholars working in various disciplines. Research scholars are encouraged to present their latest research findings highlighting the outcomes of ongoing research in frontier areas of Sciences and Technology. Further, the platform serves as a fertile ground to inspire the scholars and generate novel ideas facilitating exchange of knowledge in different fields and inculcating multidisciplinary research culture. Registration Poster Previous Next Call for poster presentations Physical Sciences Chemical Sciences Biological Sciences Medical Sciences Allied Health Sciences Engineering Law and Social Sciences Management Agriculture Humanities Research @ SRMIST In line with the mission of the institute, faculty and students at SRMIST are encouraged to carry out research in basic and applied areas of Technology and Sciences. SRMIST further fosters research in a challenging, yet rewarding manner, by providing the state of art infrastructure, seed fund, selective excellence programs and recruitment of junior research fellows providing them stipend, on par with government institutions. The areas of research span across 36 fields, including engineering & technology, science, arts, commerce, Law and medicine. Researchers associated with various disciplines contribute in their field of expertise by way of publications, patents, industrial collaborations, startups and other valuable contributions to the serve the society at large. SRMIST supports the research endeavors of faculty and students by providing incentives for publications and patents. Diversity Home Life at SRM Student Life Diversity Living and learning together It’s a ‘mini India’ out here at the SRM Institute of Science and Technology ( formerly known as SRM University ) campus – with students from all over India and even from other parts of the world. The campus too reflects this cosmopolitan aspect of the student community, with celebrations of regional festivals. Living and learning together instills self-confidence and a spirit of camaraderie among the students, and contributes to professional fraternity. Mini India on Campus Prevalence of a diverse and inclusive culture at campus enhances the learning environment by improving the ability to work in diverse groups. Each student at the campus brings his or her own unique cultural background and experience. There are several activities to keep students busy, in various areas of interest. Across the university’s four campuses – at Kattankulathur, Ramapuram and Vadapalani in Chennai and Modi Nagar in Delhi – it’s a stimulating environment for living and learning, with a diversity of students from across India and the world. Living and learning together instills self-confidence and a spirit of camaraderie among the students, and contributes to professional fraternity. Cultural activities, sports, fine arts and entrepreneurship are all nurtured here, encouraging students to venture beyond the realms of academics.

National Cadet Corps (NCC) The objective of National Cadet Corps is to develop character, comradeship, and capacity for leadership among youth in India. Home National Cadet Corps (NCC) NCC Overview The objective of the National Cadet Corps is to develop character, comradeship, and capacity for leadership among youth in India. NCC at SRM motivates the students with the best training and the ideals of selfless service. The training inculcates team spirit, a sense of nationalism, and it also helps in developing the overall personality of an individual. The organization is under the command of Col. Prof. C. Muthamizchelvan, Vice Chancellor of SRMIST. The SRM NCC Senior Division (SD) Army Wing was established in August 1998, followed by the Senior Wing (SW) in 2005. The sub-unit of SRMIST is affiliated with the 1 (TN) Signal Company NCC, Madras Group ‘A’, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Andaman and Nicobar Directorate. The SRM NCC Navy Wing for both Senior Division and Senior Wing was established in 2021. This sub-unit of SRMIST is affiliated with the 4 (TN) Naval Technical Unit, Madras Group ‘B’, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Andaman and Nicobar Directorate. Meet Our Officers Meet Our Senior Under Officers Achievements Announcements Aims Of NCC To Develop Character, Comradeship, Discipline, Leadership, Secular Outlook, Spirit of Adventure, and Ideals of Selfless Service amongst the Youth of the Country. To Create a Human Resource of Organized, Trained and Motivated Youth, To Provide Leadership in all Walks of life and be Always Available for the Service of the Nation. To Provide a Suitable Environment to Motivate the Youth to Take Up a Career in the Armed Forces. Objectives Of NCC To fulfill the requirements expected in the latest socio-economic scenario in India. To develop character, discipline, comradeship, secular outlook, ideals of selfless services and adventure spirits amongst large numbers of young citizens. To create a pool of trained, organized and motivated youth with various leadership qualities in every walk of their life and serve the Nation irrespective of the career they choose. To provide a sound environment conducive towards motivating large numbers of young people of India to join armed forces. Orientation 2024 The National Cadet Corps (NCC) at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) usually holds an orientation program to introduce new cadets to the NCC’s objectives, rules, and activities. The orientation covers key areas such as leadership development, discipline, physical fitness, national service, and the benefits of NCC enrollment. During the event, cadets are informed about the NCC’s training schedule, the different camps and certificates they can pursue (A, B, and C Certificates), and how it can enhance their academic and career prospects. Senior officers or NCC alumni often share their experiences to motivate new cadets. During the event, cadets are informed about the NCC’s training schedule, the different camps and certificates they can pursue (A, B, and C Certificates), and how it can enhance their academic and career prospects. School of Public Health Empowering Global Health Leaders Shaping future health leaders through innovation, research, and global impact. MHS UG - Apply Now MHS PG - Apply Now Programs Placements Alumni Pedagogy Collaborations Projects Wellness Program Newsletter Advancing Global Health Through Innovation and Education School of Public Health The School of Public Health, established in 2007, is a premier institution dedicated to advancing public health education, training, and research. The school nurtures competent and inspired public health professionals, health managers, health data scientists, biostatisticians and thought leaders through cutting edge education technology and experiential learning methods. The students here get the opportunity to participate in ongoing research projects of the school and get hands on training and field exposure. The school fosters industry interface and international collaborations. Health Data Science Master of Public Health – MPH MPH – Applied Health Research Research & Certificate Program PhD in Public Health Associate Fellow of Industrial Health Current Student Profile 6 MBBS 7 BDS 5 AYUSH Doctors 4 Engineers 11 B.Sc Public Health 43 Health Sciences Students List Dean's Message Vision Mission Dr. Hari Singh Dean School of Public Health, SRMIST We look forward to welcoming you to the School of Public Health, where students are nurtured to secure well-paid positions in the highly competitive domain of public health. At the School of Public Health, the faculty bring a wealth of field experience and relevant research into classroom, ensuring rigorous competency-based education. Through interdisciplinary coursework, hands-on learning opportunities, and collaborations with leading health organizations, we will prepare students to develop communication skills, project management skills, research and data analytics to grow professionally and contribute to improving health outcomes of populations. Our Master of Public Health (MPH) and BSc-MPH integrated programs are designed for those who are interested in designing and managing health projects. The MSc–Biostatistics and Epidemiology program is for those with a keen interest in research and data analytics. The MSc–Health Data Science program is for those interested in use of artificial intelligence, machine learning, algorithms, and cloud computing and big data analytics in healthcare domain. To be one of the most sought-after public health institutions in the country. Nurture competent and inspired public health professionals who will make meaningful contributions to improving the health of communities. Admission Procedure Applications for Master programs are invited in January each year through a notification on the Institute’s website. Interested candidates need to register on the SRM website and appear for a written examination held in April and June. Qualified candidates proceed to an interview. Upon selection, candidates are required to submit the certificates and specified fee by a given date. Classes begin by mid-July.

Experience Life Beyond the Classroom, Discover, Connect, and Thrive in a Campus that Never Sleeps Campus Life at SRMIST The School of Public Health is located near Chennai at the Kattankulathur campus of the SRM Institute of Science & Technology. The campus offers five hostels – three for girls and two for boys, where accommodation is available on a first-come, first-served basis. Due to limited availability, interested students are advised to book their accommodation immediately after enrollment. Additionally, many paying-guest accommodations are available in surrounding areas. Smart Classrooms Arts & Clubs Sports & Fitness Diversity Vibrant College Life Anti-Ragging Committee Empowering Graduates with Outstanding Career Outcomes Placement MPH graduates generally begin their professional journey with mid-level positions such as program officer or research officer in a national or international NGO, CSR initiatives, research organization, or government health department. Progressing in their careers, they ascend to state-level manager, eventually reaching national-level management positions. Some specialize in specific areas like Monitoring, Learning and Evaluation, Research, Community Mobilization, Advocacy, TB, HIV/AIDS, Family Planning, Gender Equity, Environmental Health, Health Communication, Health Economics etc. Some students choose to pursue a PhD. and get into academia. text: Faculty of Engineering & Technology Kattankulathur - Chennai Home Faculty of Engineering & Technology In the words of our Founder & Chancellor SRMIST’s engineering programs endeavor to be at the forefront of innovation. They also foster multi-disciplinary collaborations aimed at solving the most pressing global problems. Our mission is to seek solutions to global challenges by using the power of engineering principles, techniques and systems. We believe that engineers should not only possess deep technical excellence, but also nurture creativity, cultural awareness and entrepreneurial skills that come from exposure to science, business, medicine and other disciplines – all integral part of the SRM experience. Our goal is to deliver world class, solutions driven programs that inspire curiosity and generate new knowledge and discoveries. Our collaboration with over 50 of the world’s best universities and 215 corporates, strengthens our academic and research programs. More on FET Dr. Leenus Jesu Martin M Dean – FET, Kattankulathur E: Dean's Message Welcome to the College of Engineering & Technology at SRMIST! At SRMIST, we are committed to academic excellence, research innovation, and holistic student development. Our college stands at the forefront of engineering education, equipping students with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in an ever-evolving technological landscape. With world-class faculty, cutting-edge research facilities, and strong industry collaborations, we ensure that our students are prepared not only for successful careers but also to become leaders and innovators in their respective fields. Our curriculum blends theoretical rigor with hands-on experience, emphasizing experiential learning, interdisciplinary projects, and entrepreneurial thinking. Beyond academics, we nurture a vibrant campus culture that fosters creativity, critical thinking, and ethical leadership. We believe in empowering our students to take on global challenges and contribute meaningfully to society. I invite you to explore the numerous opportunities that our college offers and join us in our mission to shape the future through education and research. Dr. Leenus J Martin Dean, Faculty of Engineering & Technology SRM Institute of Science and Technology Chairs/Deans and HoDs Explore our Programs Students seeking Inter-Disciplinary programmes and flexibility in course curriculum will find just that at SRMIST. A student shall have an option of choosing any one of the following pathways (i) Major (ii) Major with Specialization (iii) Major with Minor or Major with specialization and a Minor The core philosophy that is practiced in SRMIST as part of the teaching-learning process is Inter-Disciplinary Experiential Active Learning (IDEAL) Explore Minor Programmes Multidisciplinary Projects Find Programs Department & Centers The Engineering and Technology faculty comprises 7 Schools and 22 Departments. Each school is headed by a Dean and comprises various Departments of Engineering, clubbed on the basis of their domain expertise. With students in India seeking more interdisciplinary programs and flexibility in course curriculum, SRMIST addressed this demand by completely leaving the option of program path to the students.

text: SRM School of Law Kattankulathur - Chennai Home SRM School of Law In the words of our Founder & Chancellor Law schools fulfill the noble obligation of imparting legal education to their students so that they can become competent, confident, and equipped to face effectively, all new challenges in their legal practice and research in this constantly evolving era, and concomitantly, to be committed to serving society with humanity, compassion, and humility. Law is one of the oldest, noblest, and greatest intellectual professions that demands holistic and focused education and guidance beyond the ‘four walls of the classroom’. One must know that behind each great jurist, judge, eminent lawyer, other exceptional professor, and legal professional is a strong law school and a dedicated fraternity of faculties committed to building these personalities. School of Law, SRM Institute of Science & Technology is one of the leading Law Schools in India recognized by BCI which was initiated with a commitment to delivering the highest standards in legal education and training. SRMIST has consistently ranked amongst the top-ranking multi-stream universities in India and abroad, with a vision to impart quality education that ensures the products of Law school stand par excellence. School of Law, SRMIST offers quality legal education in Undergraduate (UG), Postgraduate (PG), Doctor of Philosophy (PhD), and Diploma programs as well as Certificate Courses and Value Added Courses which are focused on developing legal knowledge in a wide variety of legal areas. SRM School of Law takes pride in developing and executing a strong curriculum and pedagogies that have been framed; keeping up contemporary standards and expectations. Placements and internships are strongly facilitated, due to which, the alumni of SRM School of Law have made us proud by occupying coveted positions in different fields. The strive towards excellence of SRM School of Law is also evident in the institution’s constant endeavor of building a team of committed and qualified teachers who are equipped to impart knowledge and training to the students such that they excel not only in academics but all spheres of life. SRM School of Law has a rich legacy, having completed a decade of its establishment, and making significant strides towards excellence. SRM School of Law was inaugurated in 2014 by the Honorable Chief Judge of the Supreme Court of Maryland, Mary Ellen Barbara along with the judicial delegates from the State of Maryland, USA which was led by the First Lady of State, Katie O’Malley. The School ties up with many foreign universities and institutions, which incorporates a rich exchange of knowledge and research activities. SRM Law School conducts two National-level Moot Court competitions annually in which students from different parts of the country take part. The industry-academic interface is a major part of teaching pedagogy. Today’s lawyer is supposed to have exposure to different disciplines like science & technology, from medicine to literature, and from arts to sports, SRMIST is a leader in all!
