I believe in the vision of SRM School of Law as an institution of academic excellence having tremendous potential to be among the best in the country. In addition, I believe that SOL will also be an institution committed to community service instilling in its students the values of professional ethics, social responsibility, and inclusiveness in addition giving them legal knowledge and skills necessary for success in their chosen career paths. The vision of SOL is to maintain high academic standards, develop innovative pedagogy, and industry-linked global curriculum. SOL aims to impart knowledge but inculcate values so that our students can contribute towards achieving social, political and economic justice. I am sure that students of SOL will make their place in the country by their legal acumen, social sensitivity, and their commitment to promote constitutional morality of the Constitution of India with guidance from the teachers and support from the non-teaching staffs. text: Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM) Home Research Research Wings Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM) Overview Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM) is a prestigious school of SRM Institute of Science and Technology which was established in 2008 to promote research in the field of traditional system of Indian Medicine, including Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani. IIISM is functioning in joint collaboration with Banaras Hindu University in the development of herbal based Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani medicine. It has also signed MoU with AYUSH and Central Council for Research in Ayurveda and Siddha (CCRAS) for promoting research in Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani system of medicine. Further, 200 acres of land has been allocated specifically for the purpose of micro propagation of medicinal plants, reiterating SRMIST’s commitment in establishing a world-class research facility to validate and standardize herbal based medicinal products. Also one acre of land at the University campus has been allocated to establish AYUSH Research Centre with the financial support of the ministry of AYUSH. Dr. R. C. Satish Kumar Phone: +91-044-27455818, +91-9884845887 E-mail: Welcome to IIISM Our warm welcome to SRMIST’s prestigious Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM). It was established in 2008 to promote research in the field of traditional system of Indian Medicine, including Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani. The encouragement and vision of our Founder & Chancellor Dr. T R Paarivendhar is a guiding force for us. IIISM is functioning in joint collaboration with Banaras Hindu University in the development of herbal based Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani medicine. We have ongoing MoUs with AYUSH & CCRAS. This is a great platform for our students, researchers and faculty members to work together on trendsetting research and experiments. text: Outward Mobility Home International Relations Outward Mobility Outward / Study Abroad We believe that our students and faculty members deserve to benefit from enhanced academic experience at SRMIST. It is with this objective in mind that we have invested considerable resources and ongoing efforts to accrue to them unparalleled pan-India and global exposure. We would like our students to make the best use of their time with SRMIST and therefore bring the world to them, quite literally. Learning now transcends national boundaries. In a world that is increasingly interconnected and interdependent, it is only fitting that a pioneering institution of higher learning like SRMIST provides opportunities to interact, work together, exchange and collaborate with counterparts across the world. This is possible through the outward mobility platform handled by us at International Relations at SRMIST and this covers mechanisms like semester abroad programme, dual degree programme and twinning program. Onboarding Ambassadors Semester Abroad This initiative from SRMIST is a first of its kind and is highly appreciated by students. So far, 650 meritorious students have been given the opportunity of a lifetime to spend a semester at some of the best universities in the world such as – Carnegie Mellon, Northeastern University, SUNNY Buffalo, University of Wisconsin, UC Berkeley, George Mason, New Hampshire, Lille Catholic University, University of Western Australia, Western Ontario, Queen’s University and many others. Explore Twinning Programme Twinning / Transfer programs allow SRM to offer Bachelors (UG) and Masters (PG) degree programs which will have their regular curricula. The existing curricula may be supplemented by additional curricula of the partnering universities if desired by SRM. Through twinning / transfer programs, SRM students will be able to earn several credits during the course of their normal study in SRM. Students will have the opportunity either to complete their studies in SRM or to transfer their credits to the partnering universities at any time after completing two year of their studies in SRM. Students, who opt for transfer, can then finish their degree programs by completing the remainder of credits in residence at the partnering universities. Explore Dual Degree Programme A dual degree programme involves a student working for two different university degrees in parallel, either at the same institution or at different institutions, may be in different countries, completing them in less time than it would take to earn them separately. The two degrees might be in the same subject area or in two different subjects. The dual degree concept is more popular in European Union, as the gaining of qualifications from more than one country is seen as an advantage in the European labour market. India, after opening its higher education to private players, is seeing a growth in the dual degree programmes with foreign universities. Explore Best of both Worlds SRM University presents an opportunity to experience the global education. India, after opening its higher education to private players, is seeing a growth in the dual degree programmes with foreign universities. Explore Best of both Worlds SRM University presents an opportunity to experience the global education. The University sponsors advanced learning for interested students, through its links with foreign universities – helping them experience an international education and a different culture – thereby raising their caliber to be at par with the best in the world. 90 + 600+ Students Sponsored for SAP 90 + MoUs with Worldwide institutions 90 + Members of IAB from 8 Countries 90 + Universities Linkages from 34 Countries Credit Transfer arrangement for Programs pursued abroad. International Advisory Board Home International Relations International Advisory Board International Advisory Board Back in 2003, SRM set up an International Advisory Board (IAB) consisting of 31 Distinguished Deans, Directors and professors from top universities across the world. SRM’s IAB is the first across the Indian University system. IAB works towards helping SRMIST set Global Standards and Internationalize through bringing globally acclaimed curriculum, research and teaching methodology to its students and faculty members. Now over 50+ Members are on IAB and they are from the best of the universities across the world including Harvard Business School, Kennedy Public School (Harvard University), MIT, Stanford, LSE, UC Berkeley, Cambridge, NUS among others. text: Accreditations & Rankings Home About Accreditations and Rankings Accreditations Accredited by NAAC with A++ Grade University Grants Commission Ministry of Education The Insitution of Engineering and Technology Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET National Board of Accreditation (NBA) Indian council of Agricultural Research(ICAR) NAAC A++ Accredited SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly SRM University) has been accredited with the highest grade A++ in its 4th Cycle by NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) for the period 16.06.2024 – 15.06.2031 (7 years). It is another feather on SRMIST’s cap as it prides over being awarded the highest grade for three consecutive cycles dating back to 2013. Certificate University Grants Commission SRMIST has been placed under Category I University by UGC / Ministry of Education as per the UNIVERSITY GRANTS COMMISSION [CATEGORIZATION OF UNIVERSITIES (ONLY) FOR GRANT OF GRADED AUTONOMY] REGULATIONS, 2018 By becoming Category I University with 12 B Status, SRMIST is in elite group of 2 Central Universities, 12 State Universities and 11 Deemed to be Universities in India. In Tamil Nadu, only three Universities, including SRMIST, have been granted this rare distinction. D. Heidel 1, D. Hochbaum 1, J. M. Sussman 2, V. Singh 1, M. E. Bahlke 1, I. Hiromi 1, J. Lee 1, and M. A. Baldo 1 1 Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, Massachusetts 02139, USA. 2 Department of Material Science and Engineering, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, Massachusetts 02139, USA. (Received 1 February 2011; accepted 30 March 2011; published online 16 May 2011) Abstract: We enhance the efficiency of heterojunction organic solar cells by introducing a thin interfacial layer between the acceptor and donor layers. The interfacial layer energy levels are chosen to provide a gradient for charges crossing the interface, approximating a conventional p-n junction with three organic semiconductors. Devices with interfacial layers exhibit increased open circuit voltage (Voc) and increased short circuit current (Jsc). The increase in Voc is due to a reduction in dark current and charge recombination. The increase in Jsc is correlated with an increase in the conversion efficiency of excitons originating in the donor or acceptor layers. The interfacial layer destabilizes charge transfer states at the donor-acceptor interface, yielding reduced exciton recombination. The introduction of thin interfacial layers may prove to be an important probe of the physics of exciton separation in organic photovoltaic cells. VC 2011 American Institute of Physics. Complaints & Redressal Home Life at SRM Complaints & Redressal Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC) SRMIST has constituted a Committee for handling and solving the grievance to express constructive & genuine concerns/grievances. To promote and maintain a sustainable, unbiased & Positive Educational Environment. It involves a process of investigation in which ‘Student’s Grievance Redressal Committee’ enquires and analyses the nature and pattern of the grievances in a strictly confidential manner. UGC Regulations, 2023 Definition Grievance Redressal Cell SRMIST has constituted a Cell for handling and solving the grievance to express constructive & genuine concerns/grievances. Grievance Portal Internal Committee The committee’s primary responsibilities include handling complaints, conducting inquiries, and organizing gender sensitization programs. It operates in accordance with the UGC (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal of Sexual Harassment of Women Employees and Students in Higher Educational Institutions) Regulation, 2015. The ICC plays a crucial role in fostering a safe and inclusive environment for employees and students, ensuring that incidents of gender-based harassment are effectively addressed and prevented. Internal Committee Women's Grievance Redressal Committee SRMIST established a Women’s Grievance Cell to address any grievances of the female students, staff, and faculty. We are committed to provide a fully confidential and supportive environment to all the female members of our campus community. Women's Grievance Redressal Committee

SRMIST Startup Ecosystem SRMIST Startup Ecosystem a dynamic and nurturing environment designed to transform innovative ideas into successful ventures. At SRMIST, we believe in fostering entrepreneurial spirit and providing our students, faculty, and alumni with the resources and support they need to turn their visions into reality. Home SRMIST Startup Ecosystem About SRMIST Startup Ecosystem The SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) Startup Ecosystem fosters innovation and entrepreneurship by providing a comprehensive support system for budding entrepreneurs. Our state-of-the-art incubation centers offer essential resources, while our extensive mentorship network connects startups with industry experts. We facilitate access to funding through venture capital, angel investors, and grants. Regular workshops and training sessions equip entrepreneurs with vital skills. Startups benefit from collaboration with our esteemed faculty and researchers, driving cutting-edge solutions. SRMIST has a proud history of nurturing successful startups recognized nationally and internationally. Whether you’re an entrepreneur, mentor, or investor, join us to drive innovation and create impactful solutions. 0 + Startups Incubated 0 + Patent Granted 0 + Cr Fund Raised 0 + Employment Generated 0 + Industry-Academia Partnership Graduated Startups Current Startups Abhinava Rizel Private Limited Abhinava Rizel developed EV motors based on a proprietary hybrid topology radial flux architecture between SynRM (Synchron Reluctance Motors) and PMSM (Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors).
